ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, PLAINTIFF HAROLD HUDSON MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-14-276292 WITH CASE CGC-13-276135 FOR ALL PURPOSES INCLUDING TRIAL. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS MODIFIED: PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS FOR ALL PURPOSES INCLUDING TRIAL IS GRANTED. ACTIONS NOS. 276135 AND 276292 ARE CONSOLIDATED FOR ALL PURPOSES INCLUDING TRIAL. HENCEFORTH ALL PAPERS SHALL BE FILED IN THE LEAD CASE, NO. 276135. NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE TRIAL SETTING CONFERENCE IS SET ON APRIL 16, 2015. THE AUGUST 20, 2015 TRIAL SETTING CONFERENCE, PREVIOUSLY SET IN THE PARMLEY CASE (#276292), IS ORDERED VACATED. calendar.matters:
ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-01-2017 Off Calendar

ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-06-2017 Continued To Dec-07-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503

Notice Of Motion For Order To Apportion And Distribute Funds
Off Calendar Mar-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for order to apportion and distribute funds will be heard at 9:00a.m. in Department 514, Hon. Stephen M. Murphy presiding. Henceforth, all asbestos apportionment motions will be heard in Department 514 and such motions shall be filed in accordance with San Francisco Uniform Local Rules of Court, Rule 8.1(B)(5). Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, matter not reported (503/CML)

Plaintiffs/Claimants Motion For Order To Apportion And Distribute Funds
Off Calendar Mar-21-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Motion for Order to Apportion and Distribute Funds.
Dept. 514, Judge: Stephen M. Murphy, Clerk: J. Bertelsen. Proceedings reported by: Mitch Lyon, CSR#6840.
Amir Sarreshtehdary, Esq., appeared for the Plaintiffs MACI PARMLEY as Successor In Interest to HAROLD HUDSON, Deceased; and VIOLA FURTADO, HELEN HUDSON, and SHANNON VANCORBACH, as Wrongful Death Heirs of HAROLD HUDSON, Deceased.
Witnesses MACI PARMLEY and VIOLA FURTADO appeared and were sworn and testified.
Witness HELEN HUDSON appeared by CourtCall, and was sworn and testified.
No appearance by Ruby O'Donnell, Shannon Van Corbach, Jerry Hudson, Jr., Norman Hudson, Michael Reimche, or Jeffrey Reimche.
After hearing the testimony and considered the evidence both oral and documentary, and good cause appearing, the Court ordered for distribution of all net settlements funds derived from plaintiffs' survival/wrongful death lawsuit and claims against asbestos bankrupt defendant trusts negotiated after the August 22, 2013 death of Harold Hudson, in the following proportions:
Estate of HAROLD HUDSON 58%
Estate of CAROL REIMCHE as wrongful death heir 4%
VIOLA FURTADO as wrongful death heir 10%
HELEN HUDSON as wrongful death heir 10%
RUBY O'DONNELL as wrongful death heir 10%
SHANNON VAN CORBACH as wrongful death heir 4%
JERRY HUDSON, JR. as wrongful death heir 4%
NORMAN HUDSON as wrongful death heir 0%

The Signature of plaintiff MACI PARMLEY alone is sufficient to bind the estate and wrongful death heirs on all settlement documents.
Order was submitted to and signed by the Court.

MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-14-276292 WITH CASE CGC-13-276135
Off Calendar Jan-07-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Maci Parmley, et al. in actions #276135 and #276292

RULING:
PLAINTIFF HAROLD HUDSON MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-14-276292 WITH CASE CGC-13-276135 FOR ALL PURPOSES INCLUDING TRIAL

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Plaintiffs' motion to consolidate actions for all purposes including trial is granted. Actions nos. 276135 and 276292 are consolidated for all purposes including trial. Henceforth all papers shall be filed in the lead case, no. 276135. No opposition filed. The Trial Setting Conference is set on April 16, 2015. The August 20, 2015 Trial Setting Conference, previously set in the Parmley case (#276292), is ordered vacated. One order signed in open court for both cases. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)
